title: 10 best herbal teas for gastroenteritis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gastroenteritis are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. These teas can have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe an irritated gastrointestinal tract and promote recovery. Examples of herbal teas that can help with gastroenteritis include chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and licorice root tea. For instance, chamomile tea can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, while ginger tea can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. Peppermint tea can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce abdominal pain, and licorice root tea can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can find relief from the unpleasant symptoms of gastroenteritis and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with gastroenteritis because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with the condition. The menthol content in peppermint also helps to soothe the stomach lining and ease inflammation, calming nausea and vomiting. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce gut inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain, making it a natural remedy for alleviating discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peppermint tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce muscle spasms, which can alleviate cramps and abdominal pain associated with gastroenteritis.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can decrease symptoms such as diarrhea, bloating, and stomach discomfort.
Carminative property: Peppermint tea's volatile oils can help to break down and release trapped gas in the digestive system, reducing symptoms of flatulence, bloating, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, peppermint roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of menthol, peppermint rhyzomes effectively calm digestive spasms and ease abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured peppermint leaves with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, or until the desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweetening with honey if desired, and enjoying within two hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with gastroenteritis because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The apigenin and bisabolol present in chamomile calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastroenteritis. Additionally, chamomile's relaxing effects on the gastrointestinal tract help to ease symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, allowing the body to recover more efficiently from the infection. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular choice for soothing the stomach and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: German Chamomile contains apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of gastroenteritis.

Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping, bloating, and diarrhea associated with gastroenteritis.

Calming and sedative property: German Chamomile's sedative effects can help soothe an upset stomach, reduce nausea and vomiting, and promote relaxation, which is essential for gut healing and recovery from gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing apigenin and other flavonoids, roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Flowers: Rich in azulene and other terpenes, flowers calm gut spasms, reduce nausea, and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for a relaxing brew weighing about one ounce per quart.
Combine one teaspoon of loose-herb chamomile flowers with boiling water in a teapot or infuser for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes releasing the soothing essential oils and flavonoids into the tea.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids after pouring.
Enjoy the calming and anti-inflammatory tea hot or let it cool to room temperature savoring the gentle chamomile flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with gastroenteritis because its calming properties soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The antispasmodic effects of lemon balm help to ease cramping and spasms in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced symptoms such as bloating and gas. Additionally, lemon balm's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds may help to combat the underlying infection causing gastroenteritis, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat the bacterial and viral infections that cause gastroenteritis.

Soothing digestive tract irritation: The flavonoids present in lemon balm tea have a calming effect on the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and soothing symptoms such as diarrhea, cramping, and bloating.

Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea's antispasmodic effects can help relax the muscles of the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation associated with gastroenteritis.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves possess carminative properties, which help to ease flatulence, bloating, and cramps often experienced during gastroenteritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves for optimal potency and flavor.
Measure two teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbal mixture for seven to ten minutes to allow flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Serve hot or chilled, sweetened with honey if desired, for a soothing and calming brew.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint teas helps with gastroenteritis because its natural ingredients, particularly menthol, can help soothe and calm the digestive system. The cooling properties of menthol can reduce inflammation and ease cramps in the stomach, while also alleviating nausea and vomiting. Additionally, spearmint's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce diarrhea and abdominal pain associated with gastroenteritis, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Spearmint tea helps to reduce bloating, cramps, and gas associated with gastroenteritis by reducing muscle spasms in the digestive tract.

Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids present in spearmint tea have been shown to relax the muscles of the stomach and intestines, providing relief from abdominal pain, cramps, and diarrhea common in gastroenteritis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Spearmint tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, fever, and chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, spearmint roots can help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut.
Rhyzomes: With its natural antibacterial agents, the rhyzome of spearmint helps combat harmful bacteria that may contribute to gastroenteritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves from your garden or store-bought dried peppermint in a clean container for later use.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried spearmint or four to six sprigs of fresh spearmint per eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured spearmint leaves in a teapot or infuser for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes depending on desired tea strength and flavor profile.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and enjoy hot or iced with honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with gastroenteritis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, nausea, and diarrhea. Additionally, licorice tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate gastroenteritis, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce inflammation in the gut, which can alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and nausea associated with gastroenteritis.
Flavonoids: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by infection or irritation, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Saponins: Possesses antimicrobial properties that can help combat the underlying infections causing gastroenteritis, such as viruses or bacteria, thereby shortening the duration of illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried licorice root extract or whole roots in amounts of one teaspoon per cup.
Grind the dried licorice root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Heat water to a temperature of 212°f and pour it over the powdered licorice root in a teapot or mug.
Steep the tea mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse and combine.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or bottle for immediate consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena teas helps with gastroenteritis because of its soothing properties that calm the digestive system. The tea's natural antispasmodic compounds reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviating symptoms such as cramps, diarrhea, and nausea. Additionally, verbena tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce excess mucus production, easing the discomfort associated with gastroenteritis. Its gentle, non-irritating nature also makes it an excellent choice for soothing the stomach lining, promoting a speedy recovery from the illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Verbena tea contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which helps alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea associated with gastroenteritis.
Antioxidant activity: Verbena's high antioxidant content helps protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of complications like bleeding and infection.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial compounds can help eliminate harmful bacteria, viruses, and parasites that cause gastroenteritis, promoting a faster recovery and preventing re-infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Verbena roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to soothe digestive issues and calm inflammation, making them effective in treating symptoms of gastroenteritis.
Bark: The bark of the verbena plant contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease pain, making it a natural treatment for digestive discomforts associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers for use in tea preparation weighing approximately 2-3 tablespoons.
Combine the harvested herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or cup at a ratio of one teaspoon per cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbs to release their natural oils and flavors into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids retaining the flavorful liquid.
Serve the herbal verbena tea hot or iced sweetened with honey to taste if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort teas helps with gastroenteritis because its antispasmodic properties soothe digestive discomforts such as cramps, spasms, and diarrhea. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the gut, calming irritated tissues and relieving symptoms like abdominal pain and nausea. Additionally, mugwort's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying infection, promoting the body's natural healing process and reducing the severity of gastroenteritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Mugwort tea's astringent property helps to reduce diarrhea and alleviate loose stools by contracting the intestines and absorbing excess fluids, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastroenteritis.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in mugwort tea help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), common comorbidities with gastroenteritis.

Antimicrobial property: Mugwort tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial or viral infections that cause gastroenteritis, such as E. coli, Salmonella, and Norovirus, by inhibiting the growth of these pathogens and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Bark: Containing tannins, which can aid in reducing inflammation and soothing gastrointestinal irritations.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried mugwort leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured mugwort with the boiling water in a large ceramic or glass teapot.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes to allow the flavors and medicinal properties to release.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea hot, with or without honey or lemon, as desired, within two hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with gastroenteritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an upset stomach and calm digestive issues. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, making it easier for the body to recover from symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, ginger has natural antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial or viral infections that may be contributing to gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger tea helps stimulate digestive enzymes to break down proteins, carbohydrates, and fats, which can alleviate symptoms of nausea, bloating, and discomfort in patients with gastroenteritis.

Anti-Inflammatory Agent: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut lining, which can relieve symptoms of diarrhea, stomach pain, and cramping.

Gastroprotective Agent: Ginger tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties may also help protect the stomach lining from further damage caused by the infection, reducing the risk of complications such as bleeding or perforation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, which is the underground stem of the ginger plant, is often used to make medicinal teas for gastroenteritis due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain compounds that have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in treating symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of fresh or dried ginger root and chop it into small pieces.
Heat one cup of water in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat for five minutes.
Add the chopped ginger to the boiling water and reduce heat to low for seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug and discard solids.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional three minutes before serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turkish rhubarb teas helps with gastroenteritis because it contains aloe-emodin, a potent anthraquinone that acts as a natural laxative, helping to ease constipation and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe the gut lining, reducing diarrhea and cramping associated with gastroenteritis. Additionally, Turkish rhubarb teas has been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria, such as E. coli, that can cause gastrointestinal infections.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds have laxative and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve symptoms such as constipation, diarrhea, and abdominal pain associated with gastroenteritis.
Fiber content: The high fiber content in Turkish Rhubarb tea helps to regulate bowel movements, soften stool, and reduce the risk of dehydration by slowing down water absorption in the gut.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, soothe gastrointestinal irritation, and protect against oxidative stress caused by gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anthraquinone glycosides, roots are effective in stimulating bowel movements and reducing inflammation in the gut.
Rhyzomes: Rich in sennosides, rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried turkish rhubarb root powder per cup of boiling water.
Pour the measured powder into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Add 200-250 milliliters of filtered boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with sugar to taste, or with honey for added flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with gastroenteritis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with the condition. The herb's mucilages also help to protect and heal the lining of the stomach and intestines, which can become damaged during an episode of gastroenteritis. Additionally, plantain teas have antibacterial properties that may help to combat the underlying infection causing the illness, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as aucubin and plumbagin, which help to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, including diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Antibacterial properties: Plantain tea has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties against various pathogens that can cause gastroenteritis, helping to combat infections and promote healing of the gastrointestinal tract.

Soften and protect mucous membranes: Plantain tea's soothing and protective effects on mucous membranes can help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the gut, making it a gentle and effective treatment for symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and cramps associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and infection in the digestive tract.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help soothe and calm digestive issues such as diarrhea and stomach cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantain leaves from your garden or a trusted source.
Wash the plantain leaves thoroughly with cool running water and dry.
Chop the plantain leaves into small pieces using scissors or a knife.
Steep the chopped plantain leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastroenteritis with herbal teas is by consuming soothing teas made from chamomile, ginger, or peppermint. These teas can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal cramps. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties, ginger can reduce inflammation and nausea, while peppermint can soothe the digestive tract. To prepare, pour boiling water over the herbs, steep for 5-10 minutes, strain, and sip slowly. Avoid adding sugar or milk, as they can worsen diarrhea. Always consult a healthcare professional for severe or persistent symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastroenteritis that are treated with herbal teas include indigestion, nausea, and diarrhea. Herbal teas like chamomile, peppermint, and ginger tea are known for their soothing effects on the digestive system. Chamomile tea is often used to treat inflammation and spasms in the stomach, while peppermint tea can help relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Ginger tea is a popular remedy for nausea and vomiting, and can also help reduce inflammation in the gut. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas to treat any medical condition.
